topic: MCP Gateway Tools Comparison 2026: Top 10 Tools for Enterprise AI Agent Workflows

key_points: Ranks 10 MCP gateways with deep-dive feature breakdowns for developers
Composio highlighted as most balanced developer-first option with 850+ pre-built integrations
Covers pricing tiers: Free (20K calls/month) through Enterprise (custom)
Emphasizes SOC2/ISO certification and zero data retention architecture
Covers Bifrost, TrueFoundry, MintMCP, MCPX, and Peta as leading enterprise options
Bifrost highlighted as best combined LLM + MCP gateway in Go with ~20µs overhead at 5,000 RPS
TrueFoundry delivers ~3-4ms latency and handles 350+ RPS on 1 vCPU
Peta positioned as '1Password for AI Agents' with server-side encrypted vault
Focuses on open-source MCP gateway options: Bifrost, Docker, IBM ContextForge, Lasso, Obot
MCPX (Lunar.dev) provides single governed entry point for all agent-to-tool interactions
Open-source options allow self-hosting with full code access and no vendor lock-in
Kubernetes-native Obot backed by $35M seed funding as complete MCP platform
Kong AI Gateway 3.12 (Oct 2025) adds AI MCP Proxy plugin translating MCP to HTTP
Allows organizations already running Kong to leverage existing API infrastructure
AI MCP OAuth2 plugin centralizes authentication across all MCP servers
Built on Nginx-based core with token-based rate limiting and semantic caching
Security-focused comparison covering authentication, access control, and audit trails
Cloudflare Zero Trust integration cited as strongest option for access control
Covers commercial gateways with SSO, catalogs, and threat detection
Gartner projects 75% of API gateway vendors will have MCP features by 2026

stats.fact: MCP ecosystem has 97M+ monthly SDK downloads and 16,000+ active MCP servers as of early 2026
Gartner projects 75% of API gateway vendors will have MCP features by 2026
Remote MCP servers are up nearly 4x since May 2025; 80% of the most-searched MCP servers offer remote deployment
Bifrost adds ~20 microseconds of overhead at sustained 5,000 RPS, making it the lowest-latency open-source gateway option
TrueFoundry AI Gateway delivers ~3-4ms latency and handles 350+ RPS on a single 1 vCPU
86% of enterprises report needing technology upgrades to deploy AI agents, driving MCP gateway adoption
Composio pricing: Free (20K tool calls/month), Standard ($29/month, 200K calls), Professional ($229/month, 2M calls)
Obot MCP Gateway is backed by $35M in seed funding as a complete open-source MCP platform including catalog, chat client, and agent orchestration

angles: Security-first framing: MCP gateway as the 'zero trust layer' between AI agents and enterprise tools — covering auth, audit trails, and threat detection
Open-source vs commercial: Bifrost/ContextForge/Obot (full code access, self-hosted) vs Composio/MintMCP/TrueFoundry (managed, faster to deploy) — the real trade-offs
Latency benchmark angle: Bifrost's 20µs vs TrueFoundry's 3-4ms vs Cloudflare's edge — which matters for your agent loop speed?
Existing infrastructure reuse: Kong AI Gateway 3.12 lets teams add MCP to existing API stacks — no greenfield required
The 'credential vault' gap: why Peta's encrypted vault model addresses the #1 enterprise AI security concern in 2026
Composio as the integration shortcut: 850+ pre-built tools vs building your own MCP servers — ROI calculation for dev teams
Cloudflare's edge play: deploying MCP servers as Workers with built-in rate limiting, auth, and D1/R2/KV bindings — serverless gateway architecture
keywords: mcp gateway rate limiting access control
